verses.text: Joshua rose early, removed from Shittim, came to Jordan, lodged there before they passed over. Guidance: Joshua rises early — the chapter's opening verb. He brings the camp to the river's edge and waits. Faith camps within sight of the impossible. The Jordan is not avoided, not reached at low tide — it is faced at flood stage (v15). The first move of crossing is to set up tents at the bank.

After three days the officers went through the host: When ye see the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God, and the priests bearing it, then remove from your place and go after it. Yet there shall be a space between you and it, about two thousand cubits: come not near unto it, that ye may know the way: for ye have not passed this way heretofore. Guidance: Two thousand cubits — about half a mile — so the whole camp could see the ark go in first. The reasoning is tender: 'ye have not passed this way heretofore.' New territory needs a clear view of where God is leading. Distance is not coldness; it is so no one misses the path.

Guidance: Two thousand cubits — about half a mile — so the whole camp could see the ark go in first. The reasoning is tender: 'ye have not passed this way heretofore.' New territory needs a clear view of where God is leading. Distance is not coldness; it is so no one misses the path.

After three days the officers went through the host: When ye see the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God, and the priests bearing it, then remove from your place and go after it. Yet there shall be a space between you and it, about two thousand cubits: come not near unto it, that ye may know the way: for ye have not passed this way heretofore. Guidance: Two thousand cubits — about half a mile — so the whole camp could see the ark go in first. The reasoning is tender: 'ye have not passed this way heretofore.' New territory needs a clear view of where God is leading. Distance is not coldness; it is so no one misses the path.

Joshua said unto the people, Sanctify yourselves: for to morrow the LORD will do wonders among you. Guidance: Before the wonder, the washing. Sanctify yourselves — wash the clothes, settle the heart, drop the grudge — because miracle work requires clean hands. God is not stingy with wonders; He waits for a people prepared to receive them without losing their soul.

Joshua spake unto the priests, Take up the ark of the covenant, and pass over before the people. They took up the ark, went before the people. Guidance: The ark goes first into a river that does not yet have a path. The priests step before the people, and the people step behind the priests. Leadership is not safe space; it is first into the water. The ark — God's seat among them — enters the danger ahead of the camp.

The LORD said unto Joshua: This day will I begin to magnify thee in the sight of all Israel, that they may know that, as I was with Moses, so I will be with thee. Thou shalt command the priests that bear the ark: When ye are come to the brink of the water of Jordan, ye shall stand still in Jordan. Guidance: God repeats His promise of chapter 1 — 'as I was with Moses, so I will be with thee' — and now visibly proves it. The Red Sea opened before Moses; the Jordan will open before Joshua. Authority is not seized; it is conferred by miracle. The priests' job is the hardest: stand still in the water. Faith often looks like standing still in a current.

The LORD said unto Joshua: This day will I begin to magnify thee in the sight of all Israel, that they may know that, as I was with Moses, so I will be with thee. Thou shalt command the priests that bear the ark: When ye are come to the brink of the water of Jordan, ye shall stand still in Jordan. Guidance: God repeats His promise of chapter 1 — 'as I was with Moses, so I will be with thee' — and now visibly proves it. The Red Sea opened before Moses; the Jordan will open before Joshua. Authority is not seized; it is conferred by miracle. The priests' job is the hardest: stand still in the water. Faith often looks like standing still in a current.

Joshua said: Come hither, hear the words of the LORD your God. Hereby ye shall know that the living God is among you, and he will without fail drive out from before you the Canaanites, Hittites, Hivites, Perizzites, Girgashites, Amorites, Jebusites. Behold, the ark of the covenant of the Lord of all the earth passeth over before you into Jordan. Take you twelve men, out of every tribe a man. As soon as the soles of the feet of the priests rest in the waters of Jordan, the waters shall be cut off and stand upon an heap. Guidance: 'The living God' — a phrase Israel will need against seven dead-idol nations. The seven tribes are named one by one to make the promise concrete. Twelve men, one per tribe, so the miracle has twelve witnesses. The priests' feet touch first, then the waters stop. Obedience precedes the dry ground.

When the people removed from their tents to pass over Jordan, and the priests bearing the ark before the people, as the feet of the priests were dipped in the brim of the water (for Jordan overfloweth all his banks all the time of harvest), the waters which came down from above stood and rose up upon an heap very far from the city Adam beside Zaretan: and those that came down toward the salt sea failed, and were cut off: and the people passed over right against Jericho. The priests that bare the ark stood firm on dry ground in the midst of Jordan, and all the Israelites passed over on dry ground, until all the people were passed clean over. Guidance: The Jordan is named as flooded — God chose the worst possible hour for the easiest crossing. The water stops twenty miles upstream at Adam, a dramatic geographic detail. Priests stand firm in the river's middle while the nation walks across. The verb 'stood firm' is given to the priests in flood, not to the people on dry ground — leaders bear the current so followers can walk dry.
